knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Micronutrients (Zn, Mn, Fe, B) essential for citrus. Adequate potassium for fruit quality.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (20-35°C) with wind-driven rain. Wounds from thorns, insects, or wind provide entry.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind-driven rain splash. Contaminated tools. Movement of infected plant material.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Exobasidium vexans) causing small translucent pale spots on young tender leaves that enlarge to form convex blisters on upper surface with concave white velvety sporulation on lower surface. Most important disease of tea.
knowledge.cure: Apply Copper oxychloride (0.2%) or Hexaconazole (0.01%) at 7-day intervals during wet weather. Pluck infected leaves during dry weather.
knowledge.prevention: Maintain proper shade level (40-60%). Avoid over-pruning that produces excessive young shoots. Ensure good drainage and aeration.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (300:100:100 kg/ha/year). Potassium for disease resistance. Zinc and magnesium supplements.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Cool humid weather (15-25°C) with intermittent sunshine and rain. Misty conditions in highlands.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind-dispersed basidiospores from blisters. Only infects young tender leaves, not mature ones.

knowledge.description: Algal disease (Cephaleuros virescens) causing orange-red velvety patches on mature tea leaves and stems. Reduces photosynthetic area and weakens branches. Not a true rust — caused by parasitic green algae.
knowledge.cure: Apply Copper oxychloride (0.2%) at onset of monsoon. Prune heavily affected branches.
knowledge.prevention: Maintain proper shade. Avoid waterlogging. Good pruning practices. Ensure adequate nutrition.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Zinc sulfate spray for bush vigor. Potassium for resistance.
knowledge.favorableConditions: High humidity with poor air circulation. Heavy shade. Weak and neglected bushes.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Rain-splashed zoospores. Spreads during wet weather.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Hemileia vastatrix) causing yellow-orange powdery pustules on lower leaf surfaces with corresponding yellow spots on upper surface. Causes severe defoliation and dieback. Has devastated coffee plantations globally.
knowledge.cure: Apply Copper oxychloride (0.25%) or Propiconazole at first pustule. Triazole-based systemic fungicides for severe outbreaks.
knowledge.prevention: Plant resistant varieties (Catimor, Sarchimor). Maintain proper shade. Balanced fertilization. Timely harvesting to reduce berry load stress.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (100:75:100 kg/ha/year). High potassium for disease resistance. Organic mulch for soil health.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid conditions (20-25°C) with rainfall. Altitude 1000-1500m. Heavy berry load stresses trees.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind and rain-dispersed urediniospores. Can travel hundreds of kilometers by wind.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Colletotrichum kahawae) causing dark sunken anthracnose lesions on green developing coffee berries. Berries turn black and mummify. Can cause 50-80% crop loss in Arabica coffee.
knowledge.cure: Apply Copper oxychloride or Chlorothalonil from initial fruit set. Repeat at 3-4 week intervals through berry expansion.
knowledge.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Proper shade management. Remove mummified berries. Timely fungicide application.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Calcium and boron for berry development. Potassium for plant vigor.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Cool wet weather (15-25°C) during berry expansion. Heavy rainfall. Altitude >1300m.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Rain-splashed conidia from mummified berries and bark lesions.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Phytophthora spp.) causing premature shedding of mature and immature leaves during monsoon season. Leaves show water-soaked lesions that turn brown/black. Repeated defoliation reduces latex yield significantly.
knowledge.cure: Aerial spraying with Mancozeb + Copper oxychloride during monsoon. Ground-level fogging in small holdings.
knowledge.prevention: Plant tolerant clones (RRII 105, RRIM 600). Maintain adequate plant spacing. Good drainage.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (10:10:10 kg/tree/year). Potassium and magnesium for latex production.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Heavy monsoon rains. High humidity >95%. Temperature 25-30°C.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Rain-splashed zoospores. Wind-dispersed sporangia during storms.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Colletotrichum gloeosporioides) causing dark brown to black spots on young leaves, blossom blight, and fruit rot. Severe in humid tropical regions during monsoon.
knowledge.cure: Apply Carbendazim (0.1%) or Mancozeb (0.25%) during flowering and fruit set.
knowledge.prevention: Prune canopy for air circulation. Remove dead wood. Use resistant varieties.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (750:325:750 g/tree/year). Organic manure. Micronutrients.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (25-30°C) with frequent rain. Monsoon season.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Rain-splashed conidia. Infected plant debris.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Taphrina maculans) causing small oval to irregular brown spots with pale centers on both leaf surfaces. Spots enlarge and coalesce causing extensive leaf blight.
knowledge.cure: Apply Mancozeb (0.25%) or Carbendazim (0.1%) at 15-day intervals during monsoon.
knowledge.prevention: Use disease-free rhizomes. Seed treatment with Mancozeb. Crop rotation. Remove crop debris.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (60:50:120 kg/ha). Organic manure (25 tonnes/ha). Neem cake as soil amendment.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid conditions (25-30°C) during monsoon season.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind and rain-dispersed spores. Infected rhizomes.

knowledge.description: Soilborne disease caused by Pythium spp. or Fusarium spp. causing rotting of rhizomes with foul smell. Above-ground symptoms include yellowing, wilting, and collapse of pseudostems.
knowledge.cure: Drench soil with Metalaxyl + Mancozeb. Treat rhizomes with Mancozeb (0.25%) for 30 minutes before planting.
knowledge.prevention: Good drainage. Seed rhizome treatment mandatory. Apply Trichoderma viride at planting. Avoid waterlogging.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Neem cake (200 kg/ha) at planting reduces soilborne pathogens.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Waterlogged soils. Heavy monsoon rains. Poor drainage.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Soilborne zoospores. Contaminated irrigation water. Infected rhizomes.

knowledge.description: Most destructive ginger disease caused by Pythium spp. (primarily P. myriotylum). Water-soaked spots appear at stem base, rhizomes become soft and mushy with foul odor. Plants yellow and collapse. Can cause 50-90% loss.
knowledge.cure: Drench affected areas with Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil MZ). Remove and destroy infected plants with surrounding soil.
knowledge.prevention: Treat seed rhizomes with Mancozeb (0.25%) for 30 minutes. Apply Trichoderma harzianum at planting. Ensure excellent drainage. Raised bed cultivation.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (75:50:50 kg/ha). Heavy organic manure (25-30 tonnes/ha). Neem cake as amendment.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Waterlogged soils during heavy monsoon. High temperature (30-35°C). Poor drainage.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Soilborne zoospores spread through water. Infected rhizomes. Contaminated tools.

knowledge.description: Bacterial disease (Ralstonia solanacearum) causing rapid wilting and yellowing of ginger plants. Milky bacterial ooze exudes from cut stems when placed in water. Rhizomes show water-soaked to dark brown internal discoloration.
knowledge.cure: No chemical cure. Remove and destroy infected plants. Treat surrounding soil with bleaching powder (20 g/m²).
knowledge.prevention: Use disease-free rhizomes from non-infected fields. Strict crop rotation (4+ years). Soil solarization. Biocontrol with Pseudomonas fluorescens.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Apply well-decomposed organic manure only. Avoid fresh manure.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm temperatures (25-35°C). Waterlogged soils. Acidic soils (pH <5.5).
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Soilborne. Contaminated irrigation water. Infected rhizomes. Tools and footwear.

knowledge.description: Viral disease (Cardamom Mosaic Virus — CdMV) causing pale green mosaic stripes along veins ('katte' means tied up in Kannada). Plants become stunted with reduced tillering and poor capsule setting.
knowledge.cure: No cure. Rogue infected plants immediately. Control banana aphid vector.
knowledge.prevention: Use virus-free planting material from certified nurseries. Remove infected plants early. Control aphid vectors with systemic insecticides.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (75:75:150 kg/ha). Organic mulch. Micronutrients (Zn, B).
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid conditions with aphid activity. Monsoon season.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Transmitted by banana aphid (Pentalonia nigronervosa). Infected rhizomes.

knowledge.description: Oomycete disease (Phytophthora meadii) causing water-soaked spots on capsules (fruits) that turn brown/black. Also causes leaf rot and stem rot. Called 'Azhukal' locally. Most damaging disease during monsoon.
knowledge.cure: Spray Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (Ridomil MZ 0.2%) or Potassium phosphonate at 15-day intervals during monsoon.
knowledge.prevention: Maintain 50% shade. Good drainage. Remove infected capsules. Bordeaux mixture (1%) prophylactic spray before monsoon.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (75:75:150 kg/ha). Potassium for disease resistance. Organic manure.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Heavy monsoon rainfall. Waterlogging. Temperature 20-28°C. Poor drainage.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Rain-splashed zoospores. Soil splashing onto capsules.

knowledge.description: Oomycete disease (Phytophthora capsici) causing rapid wilting and death of black pepper vines. Collar region shows dark water-soaked lesion. Roots and basal stem rot. Leaves droop, turn black, and fall. Can kill vines within weeks.
knowledge.cure: Drench root zone with Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (0.2%). Copper oxychloride spray on foliage. Trunk paste with Bordeaux paste.
knowledge.prevention: Ensure excellent drainage. Avoid waterlogging. Provide shade trees. Apply Trichoderma harzianum + Pseudomonas fluorescens at root zone. Trench around infected vines.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (50:50:200 g/vine/year). Potassium-heavy for vine vigor. Organic manure (10 kg/vine).
knowledge.favorableConditions: Heavy monsoon rainfall with waterlogging. Temperature 25-30°C. Poor drainage.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Soilborne zoospores. Spread through water during monsoon. Infected soil and debris.

knowledge.description: Pest damage from Pollu beetle (Longitarsus nigripennis) causing internal feeding in developing berries. Affected berries become hollow, light, and shriveled ('pollu' = hollow berry). Major cause of hollow berry in pepper.
knowledge.cure: Spray Quinalphos (0.05%) or Lambda-cyhalothrin during berry development. Two sprays at 20-day intervals from berry formation.
knowledge.prevention: Remove and destroy infested berries. Clean cultivation. Harvest early when berries start yellowing.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Adequate potassium for berry development and filling.
knowledge.favorableConditions: During berry development period (May-August). Monsoon season.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Adult beetles fly between vines. Eggs laid inside developing berries.

knowledge.description: Phytoplasma disease causing yellowing of lower leaves progressing upward, crown narrowing with short erect leaves, reduced bunch size, and tapering of trunk. Most devastating disease of arecanut in India.
knowledge.cure: No cure. Remove and destroy infected palms. Tetracycline trunk injection gives temporary remission but is not practical.
knowledge.prevention: Use disease-free seedlings from non-endemic areas. Control leafhopper vectors. Remove infected palms early to reduce inoculum.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (100:40:140 g/palm/year). Organic manure (12 kg/palm/year). Micronutrients.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Endemic in Western Ghats region. Leafhopper vector activity.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Transmitted by Proutista moesta (lace-wing bug/planthopper). Infected seedlings.

knowledge.description: Viral disease (TMV) causing light and dark green mottled mosaic on leaves, leaf curling and distortion, stunting, and reduced leaf quality. One of the first viruses ever discovered. Extremely stable and persistent.
knowledge.cure: No cure. Remove infected plants. Disinfect hands and tools with 10% trisodium phosphate or skim milk.
knowledge.prevention: Use TMV-resistant varieties. Workers must wash hands with soap before handling plants. No smoking in fields. Steam sterilize seedbeds.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Optimal nutrition reduces symptom severity.
knowledge.favorableConditions: The virus is extremely stable — survives in dried plant material for 50+ years. Any climate.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Mechanical — touch, tools, contaminated hands. NOT insect-transmitted. Present in processed tobacco products.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Macrophomina phaseolina) causing dark brown to black stem lesions at or near soil level. Stems become brittle and break. Plants wilt and die. Minute sclerotia visible on infected tissue.
knowledge.cure: Drench base of plants with Carbendazim (0.1%). Remove infected plants with roots.
knowledge.prevention: Seed treatment with Carbendazim + Thiram. Deep summer plowing. Crop rotation with rice. Apply Trichoderma viride.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (60:30:30 kg/ha). Potassium for stem strength. Avoid nitrogen excess.
knowledge.favorableConditions: High temperature (30-35°C) with drought stress followed by rain.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Soilborne sclerotia survive for years. Spread through contaminated soil and water.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Erysiphe pisi) causing white powdery growth on leaves, stems, and pods. Severe infections cause yellowing, drying, and premature senescence.
knowledge.cure: Apply wettable Sulfur (0.25%) or Karathane (0.05%). Repeat at 15-day intervals.
knowledge.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Early sowing to escape late-season mildew. Proper spacing.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Phosphorus for root development. Rhizobium inoculation for nitrogen fixation.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm dry weather (20-25°C). Late-sown crops most affected. Low humidity.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind-dispersed conidia.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Uromyces viciae-fabae var. viciae-fabae) causing orange-brown pustules on leaves, stems, and pods. Severe infection causes premature drying.
knowledge.cure: Apply Mancozeb (0.25%) or Propiconazole at first symptom. Repeat at 15-day intervals.
knowledge.prevention: Plant resistant varieties. Early sowing. Destroy crop debris after harvest.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Potassium for disease resistance. Phosphorus for root vigor.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Cool humid weather (15-20°C) with dew.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind-dispersed urediniospores.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Alternaria dauci) causing dark brown to black spots on carrot leaves and petioles. Spots coalesce causing leaf blight and die-back. Reduces root size and quality.
knowledge.cure: Apply Mancozeb (0.25%) or Chlorothalonil at first symptom. Repeat at 10-14 day intervals.
knowledge.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Crop rotation (3 years). Hot water seed treatment. Avoid overhead irrigation.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Adequate potassium for root development. Boron for root quality.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (25-30°C) with frequent rain or dew.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind and rain-dispersed conidia. Seed-borne.

knowledge.description: Phytoplasma disease causing transformation of floral parts into green leaf-like structures (phyllody). Capsules replaced by leafy shoots. Plants remain green but produce no seed. Transmitted by leafhoppers.
knowledge.cure: No cure. Rogue infected plants. Control leafhopper vectors (Orosius orientalis) with Imidacloprid.
knowledge.prevention: Early sowing. Control weed hosts. Insecticidal seed treatment. Resistant varieties where available.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (60:40:20 kg/ha). Adequate phosphorus for flowering.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm weather with active leafhopper populations. Late-sown crops.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Persistently transmitted by leafhopper (Orosius orientalis).

knowledge.description: Soilborne fungal disease (Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. ricini) causing progressive yellowing and wilting of castor plants from lower leaves upward. Vascular browning in stem cross-section.
knowledge.cure: Seed treatment with Carbendazim + Thiram. Soil application of Trichoderma viride.
knowledge.prevention: Use resistant varieties (GCH-7, DCH-519). Crop rotation (3+ years). Deep summer plowing.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (60:40:40 kg/ha). Adequate potassium. Maintain soil pH 6.0-7.0.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Soil temperature 25-30°C. Acidic soils. Continuous castor cropping.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Soilborne. Seed-borne. Contaminated soil and water.

knowledge.description: Complex disease involving multiple fungi (Fusarium spp., Aspergillus spp., Curvularia lunata) colonizing developing grain during wet weather at maturity. Grain becomes discolored, shriveled, and may contain mycotoxins.
knowledge.scientificName: Fusarium spp. / Aspergillus flavus / Curvularia lunata
knowledge.cure: Spray Mancozeb (0.25%) or Carbendazim at 50% flowering and repeat at milk stage.
knowledge.prevention: Use grain mold-resistant varieties with hard/colored grain. Timely harvesting. Avoid rainy-season maturity.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (80:40:40 kg/ha). Adequate nitrogen for grain filling. Zinc supplements.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Rain at grain filling/maturity. Temperature 25-30°C. High humidity.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Airborne spores colonize grain through exposed panicle. Rain promotes infection.

knowledge.description: Oomycete disease (Sclerospora graminicola) causing chlorotic streaks on leaves, 'green ear' — floral parts transformed into green leafy structures, and stunted growth. Called 'green ear disease'. Major disease of pearl millet in India.
knowledge.cure: No field cure. Remove green ear heads. Seed treatment with Metalaxyl (Apron 35 SD at 6 g/kg seed).
knowledge.prevention: Mandatory seed treatment with Metalaxyl before sowing. Use resistant hybrids. Crop rotation.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (60:30:30 kg/ha). Zinc application (25 kg ZnSO₄/ha). Adequate nitrogen for tillering.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Cool humid weather (20-25°C) with heavy dew. Early seedling stage most susceptible.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Soilborne oospores infect seedlings. Wind-dispersed sporangia for secondary spread. Seed-borne.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Pyrenophora teres) causing distinctive net-like pattern of brown longitudinal and transverse striations on barley leaves ('net blotch'). Also a spot form exists. Reduces photosynthesis and grain quality.
knowledge.cure: Apply Propiconazole or Tebuconazole at flag leaf stage.
knowledge.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Crop rotation. Destroy infected stubble. Seed treatment.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Adequate nitrogen for grain fill. Potassium for disease resistance.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Cool wet weather (15-25°C) with prolonged leaf wetness.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind and rain-dispersed conidia from infected stubble. Seed-borne.

knowledge.description: Viral disease (MYMV, Begomovirus) transmitted by whitefly causing bright yellow mosaic patches on leaves alternating with green areas. Severely infected plants produce few or no pods.
knowledge.cure: No cure. Rogue infected plants early. Control whitefly with Imidacloprid (0.3 ml/L) or Thiamethoxam.
knowledge.prevention: Use resistant varieties (SML-668, Pusa Vishal). Timely sowing. Border crop of maize as barrier. Yellow sticky traps.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (20:40:20 kg/ha). Rhizobium inoculation. Phosphorus for root vigor.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm weather with high whitefly populations. Kharif (rainy) season.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Persistently transmitted by whitefly (Bemisia tabaci).

knowledge.description: Viral disease causing crinkling, curling, and rugosity (puckering) of leaves with reduced leaf size. Plants become bushy with shortened internodes. Pod setting severely reduced.
knowledge.cure: No cure. Remove infected plants. Control aphid and whitefly vectors.
knowledge.prevention: Resistant varieties. Seed treatment with Imidacloprid. Border crops. Avoid late sowing.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK (20:40:20 kg/ha). Rhizobium + PSB inoculation.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather with vector activity.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Transmitted by multiple vectors including seed transmission (5-40%).

knowledge.description: Yellowing of leaves due to insufficient iron, nitrogen, magnesium, or manganese for chlorophyll production. Interveinal chlorosis (veins stay green, tissue yellows) typically indicates iron or manganese deficiency. Uniform yellowing suggests nitrogen deficiency.
knowledge.cure: Apply chelated micronutrients (iron EDDHA, manganese sulfate) as foliar spray for quick correction. Adjust soil pH if too alkaline (iron deficiency) or too acidic.
knowledge.prevention: Maintain soil pH 6.0-7.0 for optimal nutrient availability. Regular soil testing every 2-3 years. Balanced fertilization program.
knowledge.fertilizer: Chelated iron (Fe-EDDHA at 10 g/L foliar spray) + magnesium sulfate (5 g/L). Balanced NPK as base fertilizer. Adjust soil pH with sulfur (alkaline soils) or lime (acidic soils).
knowledge.favorableConditions: Alkaline soils (pH >7.5) for iron/manganese deficiency. Sandy/leached soils for nitrogen/magnesium deficiency.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: N/A — not a disease. Caused by soil nutrient imbalance.

knowledge.description: Computer vision detected moderate disease symptoms — likely blight or leaf spot complex with 10-30% leaf area affected. Pattern analysis suggests fungal origin based on spot morphology and distribution.
knowledge.cure: Apply broad-spectrum fungicide (Mancozeb 0.25% or Copper oxychloride 0.25%). Remove infected foliage. Improve air circulation.
knowledge.prevention: Crop rotation with unrelated crops. Proper spacing for air flow. Avoid overhead irrigation. Regular scouting.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK with potassium emphasis. Avoid excess nitrogen that promotes lush, disease-susceptible growth.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid conditions with poor air circulation.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind and rain-dispersed spores from infected tissue.

knowledge.description: Computer vision detected severe disease or rot affecting more than 50% of the leaf/plant area. Advanced infection stage with extensive tissue necrosis, possible secondary infections, and significant crop damage.
knowledge.cure: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread to healthy plants. Apply systemic fungicide (Carbendazim 0.1% or Metalaxyl + Mancozeb) to surrounding survivors.
knowledge.prevention: Use certified disease-free planting material. Implement strict crop rotation (3+ year cycle). Regular scouting for early detection. Maintain balanced nutrition.
knowledge.fertilizer: After recovery: balanced NPK to restore plant vigor. Foliar micronutrient spray for quick recovery. Organic amendments to improve soil health.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Extended periods of warm humid weather. Dense plantings with poor air circulation.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Multiple pathways depending on specific pathogen — wind, rain, soil, insects.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease (Alternaria spp.) causing concentric ring target-like lesions on leaves of various solanaceous crops. Begins on older/lower leaves and progresses upward.
knowledge.cure: Apply Chlorothalonil, Mancozeb, or copper-based fungicides every 7-10 days during humid weather.
knowledge.prevention: Crop rotation (2-3 years). Proper plant spacing. Water at base, not overhead. Mulching.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK. Extra potassium for disease resistance. Avoid excessive nitrogen.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm temperatures (24-29°C) with alternating wet and dry periods.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind-dispersed conidia. Survives in crop debris.

knowledge.description: Oomycete disease (Phytophthora infestans) causing fast-spreading water-soaked dark lesions with white sporangia on undersides. Highly destructive — can destroy crops in days.
knowledge.cure: Apply Mancozeb, Metalaxyl, or Copper-based fungicides immediately upon detection. Remove infected plants — do NOT compost.
knowledge.prevention: Use resistant varieties. Monitor weather conditions. Avoid overhead irrigation. Destroy volunteer plants.
knowledge.fertilizer: Phosphorus and potassium-rich fertilizer. Limit excess nitrogen that creates dense humid canopy.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Cool nights (10-15°C), warm days (15-25°C), high humidity >90%.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind-dispersed sporangia. Extremely rapid epidemic potential.

knowledge.description: Bacterial disease (Xanthomonas spp.) causing small dark raised spots with yellow halos on leaves and raised scab-like lesions on fruit.
knowledge.cure: Apply copper-based bactericides + Mancozeb every 5-7 days. Remove severely infected plant material.
knowledge.prevention: Disease-free seed and transplants. Avoid overhead irrigation. Crop rotation. Sanitize tools.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK with calcium supplements for cell wall strength.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm humid weather (24-30°C) with frequent rain.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Seed-borne. Rain splash. Wind-driven rain.

knowledge.description: Fungal disease causing white powdery coating on leaves reducing photosynthesis. Unlike most fungi, thrives in dry conditions with high humidity but no free water.
knowledge.scientificName: Various (Erysiphe spp., Podosphaera spp., Oidium spp.)
knowledge.cure: Apply sulfur-based fungicides, potassium bicarbonate, or neem oil. Remove infected plant parts.
knowledge.prevention: Good air circulation. Avoid overhead watering. Plant resistant varieties. Proper spacing.
knowledge.fertilizer: Balanced NPK with potassium emphasis. Silicon-based supplements strengthen cell wall defenses.
knowledge.favorableConditions: Warm dry days (20-30°C) with cool humid nights.
knowledge.spreadMechanism: Wind-dispersed conidia. Does not require free water on leaf surface.

knowledge.description: No disease detected. Plant appears healthy with normal leaf coloration, no spots, lesions, or discoloration visible. Continue regular monitoring and care.
knowledge.cure: No treatment needed. Continue regular care and monitoring for early signs of disease.
knowledge.prevention: Maintain good agricultural practices: proper watering, balanced fertilization, crop rotation, and regular scouting for early disease detection.
knowledge.fertilizer: Standard balanced NPK (10-10-10) fertilizer once per month during growing season. Adjust based on soil test results and crop requirements.
knowledge.favorableConditions: N/A
knowledge.spreadMechanism: N/A
